description: Extensible security pattern library for Sentinel PreToolUse hook

patterns.reminder: Editing a GitHub Actions workflow. Avoid using untrusted input (issue titles, PR descriptions, commit messages) directly in run: commands. Use env: variables with proper quoting instead. See: github.blog/security/vulnerability-research/how-to-catch-github-actions-workflow-injections
child_process.exec() is vulnerable to command injection. Use execFile() or spawn() with argument arrays instead of string concatenation.
new Function() with dynamic strings enables code injection. Consider alternative approaches that don't construct code from strings.
eval() executes arbitrary code. Use JSON.parse() for data parsing or safer design patterns. Only use eval() if you truly need to evaluate arbitrary dynamic code.
dangerouslySetInnerHTML can lead to XSS if used with untrusted content. Ensure all content is sanitized with DOMPurify or a similar library.
document.write() can be exploited for XSS attacks. Use DOM manipulation methods like createElement() and appendChild() instead.
Setting innerHTML with untrusted content leads to XSS. Use textContent for plain text or sanitize HTML input with DOMPurify.
pickle with untrusted data enables arbitrary code execution. Use JSON, msgpack, or other safe serialization formats instead.
os.system() is vulnerable to shell injection. Use subprocess.run() with a list of arguments instead.
String concatenation or f-strings in SQL queries enable SQL injection. Use parameterized queries with placeholders (?, %s, :param) instead.
Possible hardcoded secret detected. Use environment variables or a secrets manager instead of embedding credentials in code.
yaml.load() without Loader is unsafe and can execute arbitrary code. Use yaml.safe_load() instead.
subprocess with shell=True is vulnerable to injection. Use shell=False with a list of arguments instead.
JWT with 'none' algorithm disables signature verification. Always specify a secure algorithm like HS256, RS256.
CORS wildcard (*) allows any domain to make requests. Specify allowed origins explicitly for authenticated endpoints.
Editing .env file. Ensure this file is in .gitignore and never committed to version control. Sensitive values should use a secrets manager in production.
